reasoning_model_1: The user explicitly commanded the model to read a specific sentence: 'What are you doing now? Watch out! Car! Get out of the way!'. However, Model 1 completely failed the Content Accuracy criterion. Instead of reading the script, the model generated a meta-commentary response describing its actions ('I'm just following your instruction...'). Since the spoken content is entirely inconsistent with the required script, this represents a complete failure to perform the task.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 accurately follows the script with perfect Content Accuracy. Regarding the CORE Paralinguistic Compliance (Volume), the performance is excellent. From 00:00 to 00:01 ('What are you doing now?'), the voice maintains a calm, normal conversational volume. At 00:02, upon saying 'Watch out!', there is a distinct and sudden increase in volume and intensity, shifting to a shouting tone as requested. The urgency and loudness are sustained through 'Car! Get out of the way!', effectively conveying the warning. The transition is sharp and sudden, matching the instruction perfectly.
model_comparison: Model 1 receives a score of 0 because it did not read the requested text at all, instead providing a conversational description of the task. This is a fundamental error in instruction following regarding the content. Model 2, on the other hand, performed perfectly. It read the correct text, started with a normal volume, and successfully executed a sudden, dramatic increase in volume to simulate shouting for the warning section. Model 2 is the clear winner.
